findings.evidence: `rg -n "prodTensorAlgEquiv|prodTensorBialgEquiv" code roadmap` shows `prodTensorAlgEquiv` is only declared at lines 111, 134, 139 and used at line 147 to build `prodTensorBialgEquiv`; the downstream-facing code uses `MonoidAlgebra.prodTensorBialgEquiv R` at `GroupAlgebraProduct.lean:65`.
findings.fix: Make `prodTensorAlgEquiv` and `prodTensorAlgEquiv_*` private, or justify the algebra-only public target and add a single coercion/identification lemma so users are not choosing between two names for the same map.
findings.issue: `prodTensorAlgEquiv` and its parallel simp lemmas expose a duplicate algebra-level API for the same map as `prodTensorBialgEquiv`, splitting the public surface for the named bialgebra target.

summary: The new bialgebra and points APIs are characterized by generator simp lemmas, but the PR also exposes a weaker algebra equivalence that appears to be only the construction layer for the bialgebra equivalence.
